title: Shoals Technologies Q1 2026 Earnings Summary
text: Shoals delivered strong Q1 2026 results with revenue of $140.6 million, up 75% year-over-year. The company added $151 million in new orders, bringing backlog and awarded orders (BLAO) to a record $758 million, representing 18% growth. Adjusted EBITDA reached $21.1 million (56% growth), though gross margins compressed to 29.6%, slightly below expectations due to product mix, tariffs, freight costs, and facility transition inefficiencies. Management raised full-year 2026 expectations: Revenue $600M-$640M (30% growth midpoint), Adjusted EBITDA $118M-$132M (26% growth midpoint), Q2 Guidance $150M-$170M revenue; $28M-$33M adjusted EBITDA. Battery energy storage bookings totaled $75 million, with $1 million in initial AI data center revenue recognized from the ON.energy partnership project. International BLAO reached $100 million, indicating successful market diversification beyond the U.S. Management characterized 2026 as a trough year for margins due to facility transitions, expecting sequential improvement through the year and meaningful expansion in 2027. CEO Brandon Moss stated: About one-fifth of our revenue will come from new products.
